QF.id: I Codes of Conduct and Investor Protection_7

question.text: Brokers must issue a contract note to clients:

answer.text: For every trade executed, containing details of the trade including time, price, quantity, and charges
Only for trades exceeding Rs. 1 lakh
Only at the end of the month
Only if the client requests it

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Contract notes are mandatory for ALL trades and must include: trade date/time, traded price, quantity, brokerage, and statutory charges. They serve as legal evidence of the transaction. **The Trap:** There's no threshold — ALL trades require contract notes. **Cross-Exam Context:** Contract note requirements are tested in Series I and VII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Electronic contract notes are accepted and are now the standard method of delivery.
